metadata.sources: ACI 318 / ACI 332, RSMeans residential 2026, NRMCA 2026 ready-mix pricing, BBB concrete complaint patterns, ICRI concrete contractor surveys

mixDesigns.use: Driveways (mixed climates), garage slabs, residential foundations
Driveways (cold climates), heavy foundations, structural slabs — required by most modern residential codes

additives.notes: 5-7% air content. Required for ANY concrete exposed to freeze-thaw. Skipping it in cold climates causes spalling within 5-10 years.
Synthetic fibers reduce surface cracking. Common on residential slabs.

driveway_replacement_2car.label: 2-car driveway replacement (~600 sqft, 4-inch slab, 4000 PSI air-entrained)
driveway_replacement_2car.scope: Tear-out, haul-off, base prep, rebar/wire, control joints every 10 feet, broom finish, sealing.

driveway_new_2car.label: 2-car driveway new install on prepared subgrade

stamped_concrete_patio.label: Stamped concrete patio (~400 sqft)
stamped_concrete_patio.notes: Pattern, color, sealer. ~50-80% premium over plain.

foundation_slab_new_construction.label: New construction monolithic slab foundation (~1,500 sqft)

supportingComponents.notes: Skipping the base is the #1 cause of cracking. Required for any slab on grade.
Wire mesh is acceptable for sidewalks; rebar required for driveways and structural slabs.
Salt-resistant sealer in northern climates extends life 2-3x.

redFlagPatterns.explanation: Exterior concrete in a freeze-thaw climate with no air-entrained mix specified. Required by ACI 332 for any exterior residential concrete. Skipping it causes surface spalling within 5-10 winters.
Mix specified below 3500 PSI for a driveway, garage slab, or foundation. Modern residential codes require 4000 PSI minimum for these applications in most climates.
Slab with no reinforcement specified. Driveways need #3 or #4 rebar grid; sidewalks at minimum need wire mesh. Without it, the slab cracks and shifts within 1-3 years.
No control joints specified. Concrete WILL crack — control joints determine where. Without joints every 10-12 feet, the slab cracks randomly across visible areas.
Slab pour with no base preparation. 4-inch compacted gravel base is mandatory under any slab. Pouring on dirt or unprepared subgrade guarantees cracking within 1-2 years.
Interior or finished-space slab with no vapor barrier. Required by IRC for interior slabs.
Hot-weather pour without curing protocol. Concrete poured in 90F+ needs continuous moisture for 7 days minimum or it shrinks and cracks. Reputable contractors specify wet curing or cure compound.
Deposit above 25% on residential concrete is high risk. Material is delivered same-day and contractor pays at delivery. Most reputable contractors ask 10-25% deposit.
No workmanship warranty. Reputable contractors offer 1-5 years on workmanship. Hairline cracks are normal and typically excluded.
No ready-mix supplier or batch plant named. Concrete quality varies wildly between suppliers.

scopeCheckItems.label: Tear-off and disposal included if replacement

iraCredit.notes: Concrete does not qualify for federal energy credits.

stateLicensing.notes: Concrete contractors are typically licensed under the state general contractor license or a specialty concrete license.

questionsToAskContractor: What PSI mix and what's the supplier?
Air-entrained mix? (Critical in any freeze-thaw climate.)
Slab thickness and reinforcement (rebar grid or wire mesh)?
How will you prep the subgrade and what depth of compacted gravel base?
Where will control joints go and how often?
What's the curing protocol — wet cure or compound, and for how many days?
How will you handle weather conditions on pour day?
What's the workmanship warranty and what does it exclude?
